completion_summary: Resolved the chronology-analysis gaps for the eight assigned laws by grounding the repair in the source texts and chronology report: PL-038-264 follows the CRACK Act repeal-and-replacement chain, PL-038-266 carries the prior-law amendment chain, PL-040-268 remains temporary appropriations history, and the remaining laws are kept to the source-supported note or amendment posture without adding unsupported chronology claims.

laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This act completes the CRACK Act replacement by repealing titles V and VI after PL 38-263 already repealed titles I-IV.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the act at the Title 18 anchor but still carries dump and link material.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the repeal history and racketeering framework as a concise statutory note and remove the dump/link material.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act remains operative as a broad employment framework and repeals earlier employment statutes without self-expiring.

current_implementation.detail: The branch keeps the employment framework as a Title 5 note, but it should stay concise.

laws.recommended_action: Retain a concise statutory note at a Title 5 employment anchor and preserve the repeal history only.

laws.title: Public Law 38-266 | Speedy Trial Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Sections 6(a) and 6(b) directly amend prior-law text, while the remaining provisions can stay as history material.

current_implementation.detail: The current note block is still parked at the Title 18 anchor.

laws.recommended_action: Apply the SEC. 6 amendments to the prior-law chain and keep only a concise history note.

laws.title: Public Law 39-267 | Reform Sentencing Act
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 34-251 and adds ongoing sentencing rules, but the record does not resolve a safe positive-law section to rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: The branch still uses a note at the Title 18 anchor and keeps the repeal history and sentencing framework as note-only treatment.

laws.recommended_action: Keep the repeal history for PL 34-251 as a concise note; if codified later, assign a precise Title 18 placement.

laws.title: Public Law 40-268 | Big Beautiful Budget Act
laws.status_basis: This is a time-bound appropriations act; its allocations and oversight instructions are not permanent Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The act is sitting in a Title 31 anchor as a full dump, but it should not be carried as permanent Code text.

laws.recommended_action: Exclude the appropriations and oversight provisions from permanent Code text and keep only a brief history note if needed.

laws.title: Public Law 41-269 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Amendments Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Sections 2 and 3 directly amend prior-law text, including a replacement of Section III and a new Section V insertion.

current_implementation.detail: The current note lives at the Title 50 anchor, but the actual amendments are not integrated.

laws.recommended_action: Apply the prior-law section amendments and replace the full note with a concise statutory note.

laws.title: Public Law 41-270 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Corrections Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act makes section-by-section corrections to the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act of 2025 and adds technical corrections.

current_implementation.detail: The note block at the Title 40 anchor does not yet reflect the underlying section-by-section amendments.

laws.recommended_action: Apply the section amendments to the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 history chain and trim the note.

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act combines repeals, direct amendments, transfers, and conforming amendments across several existing laws.

current_implementation.detail: The current note block remains at the Title 18 anchor.

laws.recommended_action: Convert the direct amendments in Titles II-VI into the affected Code or prior-law text and keep only a concise residual note.
